pattern: Every word's capitalization is decided purely by that word's own length, independent of its neighbors, so a single pass over the split words suffices.
hints: Split the text into its individual words -- each word is judged only on its own length, never on the words around it.
A word with one or two letters becomes entirely lowercase; any longer word keeps just its first letter uppercase with every other letter lowered.
Transform each word in place with that rule, then rejoin the words with single spaces in their original order to rebuild the result.

edge_inputs.description: every word length 3+, all get capitalized
mixture including a two-letter word that must be fully lowercased
mixture including a one-letter word that must be fully lowercased
single one-letter word, minimum possible title length
already correctly capitalized input must stay unchanged (idempotence)
all-uppercase words longer than two letters
repeated short words back to back, none should be capitalized
single long word spanning nearly the whole allowed length

stress.description: random sequences of random-case words with random lengths, total length kept within the constraint
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
    words = []
    total = 0
    max_len = 100
    num_words = rng.randint(1, 20)
    for _ in range(num_words):
        sep = 1 if words else 0
        remaining = max_len - total - sep
        if remaining < 1:
            break
        wlen = rng.randint(1, min(10, remaining))
        word = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(wlen))
        words.append(word)
        total += wlen + sep
    title = ' '.join(words)
    return [title]
stress.note: num_words and word lengths are bounded so the joined title never exceeds the 100-character constraint
